description: Ferns, mosses, and other non-flowering primitive plants.
plants: Unfurling fronds in the damp shade, spreading by spores instead of seeds, older than any flowering thing.
Jointed stems stacked like tiny bamboo, a living fossil that has looked the same for millions of years.
A thin crust fused to rock or bark, so slow and patient it outlasts the surface it clings to.
Flat green lobes pressed against wet stone, so small and quiet it goes unnoticed by everything that walks past.
A soft green carpet clinging to stone and bark, quietly burying whatever it covers.
